original_hypothesis.title: Public Mint DoS via Overlapping Phase Configuration
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The `mint` function uses an `if / else if` structure to determine the active phase. If the admin configures overlapping phases (e.g., allowlist ends after public starts), the `if` condition for the allowlist phase evaluates to true during the overlap. Public minters without a Merkle proof will fall into the allowlist branch, fail the proof check, and revert, completely preventing public minting until the allowlist phase ends.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The strict `if/else if` ordering prioritizes the allowlist phase over the public phase without a fallback mechanism, making phase overlaps highly destructive to public participation.
deployment_assumptions: Admin configures overlapping allowlist and public phases
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No validation in `setCollectionPhases` to prevent `allowlistEndTime > publicStartTime`

proof_of_signal.pattern: Strict `if / else if` ordering for phase checks

preconditions: Current time is within the overlap window
Caller is not on the allowlist

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Public minters are forced into the allowlist branch and revert
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No validation of phase ordering in setter

analysis_questions: Can a public user mint during an overlap?
Is there any fallback to the public branch?
validation_requirements: Attempt to mint as a non-allowlisted user during the overlap

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: An admin configures a collection where the public sale starts 1 hour before the allowlist ends to create hype. During that hour, all public users attempt to mint but their transactions revert, causing frustration and wasted gas.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Loss of early public mint revenue and wasted user gas
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Resolves automatically when allowlist phase ends

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing tests for overlapping phase configurations

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: High revert rate on mint function

validation_result.impact_summary: When admin configures overlapping allowlist and public phases (allowlistEndTime > publicStartTime), the if/else if structure forces all callers into the allowlist branch during the overlap, causing non-allowlisted public minters to revert on Merkle proof verification.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The mint function uses a strict if/else if structure to determine the active phase. The first branch checks if `block.timestamp` is within the allowlist window. The second branch (else if) checks the public window. When `allowlistEndTime > publicStartTime`, there exists a time window where both conditions would be true, but because of the if/else if ordering, only the allowlist branch executes. Non-allowlisted users calling mint during this overlap will always enter the allowlist branch and fail the `MerkleProof.verifyCalldata` check, reverting with 'invalid proof'. The `setCollectionPhases` function has no validation to prevent this misconfiguration. This is a real code defect: the setter lacks input validation, and the mint function lacks a fallback mechanism.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof_of_signal correctly identifies MinterContract.sol lines ~220-234 as containing the if/else if phase structure. The setCollectionPhases function at lines ~170-177 confirms there is no validation of phase ordering. Both code locations directly support the hypothesis.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The if/else if structure ensures only the first matching branch executes. When allowlist and public phases overlap temporally, the allowlist branch always takes priority, preventing any non-allowlisted user from reaching the public minting branch.
No validation that _allowlistEndTime <= _publicStartTime. The function accepts any combination of timestamps, including overlapping phases that break the mint function's if/else if logic.

calling_sequence: 1. User calls mint() during the overlap window (publicStartTime <= block.timestamp <= allowlistEndTime)
2. First condition evaluates: block.timestamp >= allowlistStartTime && block.timestamp <= allowlistEndTime → TRUE
4. For non-delegated user: node = keccak256(abi.encodePacked(msg.sender, _maxAllowance, tokData))
5. require(MerkleProof.verifyCalldata(merkleProof, collectionPhases[col].merkleRoot, node), 'invalid proof') → REVERTS for non-allowlisted user
6. The else if (public phase) branch is NEVER reached
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: When allowlistEndTime > publicStartTime, during the overlap window [publicStartTime, allowlistEndTime], block.timestamp satisfies BOTH the allowlist condition (line ~220) and the public condition (line ~241). However, the if/else if structure means only the first (allowlist) branch executes. A public minter with no valid Merkle proof will always revert at the MerkleProof.verifyCalldata require statement (line ~240), and the public branch is completely unreachable during this window.

deployment_assumptions_checked: Admin configures overlapping allowlist and public phases
config_validation.deployment_evidence: The fixturesDeployment.js script deploys contracts but does not call setCollectionPhases, so no phase configuration is established in the deployment script. The setCollectionPhases function itself has no validation preventing overlapping phases.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The scenario is valid: An admin sets a collection with allowlistEndTime = T+2h and publicStartTime = T+1h (1 hour overlap). During the overlap window [T+1h, T+2h], any user not on the allowlist who calls mint() will have their transaction enter the allowlist branch and revert with 'invalid proof'. Public minting is completely blocked during this overlap despite the public phase being nominally active. This is not an adversarial attack but a code defect triggered by admin misconfiguration that has no guardrail.
validation_result.impact_assessment: MEDIUM severity. During the overlap window, all non-allowlisted users are denied access to public minting. This causes: (1) wasted gas for users who attempt to mint, (2) lost revenue from public mint sales during the overlap period, (3) user frustration and potential trust loss. The issue resolves automatically when the allowlist phase ends. The defect is in the code's failure to validate phase ordering in the setter AND its inability to handle overlapping phases in the mint function.
recommended_mitigations: Add validation in setCollectionPhases: require(_allowlistEndTime <= _publicStartTime, 'Phases overlap')
Alternatively, restructure the mint function to attempt public phase verification as a fallback when allowlist proof fails during an overlap period
Add an explicit comment or documentation warning admins about phase ordering requirements
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy all contracts using fixturesDeployment. 2. Create a collection and set collection data. 3. Call setCollectionCosts with salesOption=1. 4. Call setCollectionPhases with overlapping times: allowlistStart=now, allowlistEnd=now+2h, publicStart=now+1h, publicEnd=now+3h, merkleRoot=someRoot. 5. Advance time to now+1.5h (overlap window). 6. Call mint() from a non-allowlisted address with empty merkleProof. 7. Verify the transaction reverts with 'invalid proof' instead of succeeding as a public mint.
uncertainties: Whether any off-chain documentation or UI prevents admins from setting overlapping phases (outside code scope)
Whether the protocol team considers this an intentional design choice (allowlist always takes priority)
additional_testing_needed: Test with exact boundary values: block.timestamp == allowlistEndTime == publicStartTime
Test burnOrSwapExternalToMint which has the same if/else if pattern for phase checking
Verify the same issue exists in the burnOrSwapExternalToMint function's phase logic
